chunk_summary: Guidelines for RUTF use in SAM management, including caregiver counseling, sachet dosing, and energy targets for children based on weight, with emphasis on safe practices and follow-up care.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of RUTF in outpatient treatment of uncomplicated SAM in children over 6 months.
Learn proper caregiver counseling techniques for safe RUTF use and household management.
Apply WHO 2023 dosing framework to calculate RUTF sachet requirements based on child weight.
Recognize danger signs requiring immediate medical attention during SAM treatment.
key_facts: RUTF provides 500 kcal per sachet and is used for outpatient treatment of uncomplicated SAM.
WHO 2023 dosing framework recommends 150–185 kcal/kg/day until recovery or transition to 100–130 kcal/kg/day after severe wasting resolves.
Example dosing: 4.0–5.0 kg child = 1.5 sachets/day; 10.1–11.7 kg child = 3.5 sachets/day.
Caregivers should divide daily sachets into small feeds, offer clean water, and discard opened sachets after 24 hours.
Danger signs include new fever, fast breathing, convulsions, lethargy, repeated vomiting, worsening oedema, or inability to take RUTF.
reference_quote: UNICEF Supply Division, Ready-to-use therapeutic food specification sheet (1 sachet = 500 kcal).
document_key_topics: WHO 2023 dosing framework for SAM treatment
